question: Does the model's final answer explicitly state that Option B is correct (i.e., Fₙ₁ = Fₙ₂)?
Does the model's final answer explicitly state that Option D is correct (i.e., ω₁ > ω₂)?
Does the model correctly reject incorrect options (A and C) and select only the correct set (B and D)?
Does the reasoning correctly identify that the ball is subject to only two forces: gravity (mg) and the normal force (N), explicitly noting the rod is smooth/frictionless?
Does the model correctly apply Newton's Third Law to conclude that the force exerted by the ball on the rod is equal in magnitude to the normal force (N) exerted by the rod on the ball?
Does the model correctly establish the vertical equilibrium equation (e.g., N sinθ = mg, or equivalent based on the angle definition) showing vertical forces balance?
Does the model conclude that the normal force N depends only on constant parameters (m, g, θ) and is therefore independent of the ball's position (implying Fₙ₁ = Fₙ₂)?
Does the reasoning correctly identify that the horizontal component of the normal force provides the centripetal force required for circular motion?
Does the model correctly set up the centripetal force equation relating force to angular velocity and radius (e.g., N cosθ = mω²r)?
Does the model correctly identify that the radius of rotation at position A is smaller than at position B (rA < rB)?
Does the model derive or state the inverse relationship between angular velocity (ω) and the square root of the radius (r) (e.g., ω ∝ 1/√r or ω = √(g/(r tanθ)))?
Does the model correctly combine the geometric relationship (rA < rB) and the derived dynamic relationship to conclude that ω₁ > ω₂?
If the model generates a free-body diagram, is the gravity vector (mg) drawn pointing vertically downwards?
If the model generates a free-body diagram, is the normal force vector (N) drawn perpendicular to the rod?
If the model generates a free-body diagram, are there no incorrect extra forces (such as friction along the rod) shown?
If the model generates a diagram, is the angle θ correctly marked between the rod and the vertical axis?
If the model generates a diagram showing positions A and B, is position A depicted closer to the rotation axis (O) than position B?
If the model generates a vector addition or decomposition diagram, is the resultant net force shown pointing horizontally towards the rotation axis?
Are the generated images visually legible, with clear arrows and readable labels (e.g., mg, N, θ)?
If multiple images are generated, is the rod's orientation (angle θ) consistent across all images?
Does the visual representation of the angle θ in the diagram match the text's description (angle between rod and vertical)?
Does the visual orientation of the normal force in the diagram match the text description (perpendicular to the rod)?
Is the spatial relationship between positions A and B in the diagram consistent with the text's geometric reasoning (A closer to axis than B)?
